name: small square shield
shortDescription: A small square shield is compact, tidy, and practical, with corners that make it look more disciplined than a round buckler. It is the sort of shield a guard chooses when storage space and common sense both matter.
flavorText: “Square shields make even panic look organized.”

name: small strong round shield
shortDescription: A small strong round shield is thicker than its size suggests, built for quick defense without surrendering confidence. It sits close, moves fast, and gives the bearer the pleasant illusion that preparation was enough.
flavorText: “Small does not mean fragile when the woodworker was stubborn.”

name: Redvale officer shield
shortDescription: A Redvale officer shield bears command markings, stern red paint, and the polished wear of field authority. It is meant to be seen in formation, where one raised shield can settle more confusion than a shouted order.
flavorText: “Redvale officers carry rank where arrows can read it.”

name: long banner
shortDescription: A long banner is cloth made into identity, trailing color and allegiance above roads, halls, and marching lines. It is light in the hand but heavy in meaning, especially when people start arguing over who may carry it.
flavorText: “A banner is only fabric until someone follows it.”

name: short banner
shortDescription: A short banner is easier to carry through doorways, alleys, and crowded market ceremonies than its grander cousins. It still declares loyalty, ownership, or warning, just with better manners about wind.
flavorText: “Short banners are for messages that expect to travel.”

name: empty book
shortDescription: An empty book is a promise in binding, all clean pages and waiting ink. Merchants see a ledger, poets see a beginning, and nervous apprentices see a place where mistakes will become permanent.
flavorText: “Blank pages are valuable because they have not lied yet.”

name: Morning Glory
shortDescription: Morning Glory blossoms open with a bright, hopeful face, as if dawn itself had learned to climb a vine. Herbalists use it carefully, gardeners adore it freely, and market sellers know early light improves the price.
flavorText: “Morning Glory is a flower that believes every day deserves ceremony.”

name: Blue Brunnera
shortDescription: Blue Brunnera bears tiny sky-colored flowers above soft leaves, delicate enough to seem shy until gathered in number. It is prized for gentle arrangements, memory charms, and gardens that prefer quiet beauty over perfume.
flavorText: “Blue Brunnera blooms like a polite secret.”

name: Blue Periwinkle
shortDescription: Blue Periwinkle is a low, persistent flower with cool petals and a habit of making neglected corners look intentional. Healers, wreath-makers, and sentimental buyers all find reasons to keep it close.
flavorText: “Periwinkle does not demand attention; it repairs a place until noticed.”

name: scarce clandestine
shortDescription: Scarce clandestine is a rare bloom traded quietly, either because it is delicate, useful, or attached to stories nobody wants written on invoices. Its petals have the suspicious elegance of something gathered after dusk.
flavorText: “Some flowers are rare because they are hard to grow; others because people deny picking them.”

name: scarce lumi pedal
shortDescription: Scarce lumi pedal glows faintly along its petal edges, a soft botanical light valued by night gardeners and subtle charm-workers. It wilts badly under careless heat, making patience part of its price.
flavorText: “Lumi petals are small lanterns that dislike being rushed.”

name: Bellflower
shortDescription: Bellflower hangs in gentle cups of color, shaped like little bells that never quite ring. It is a favorite for cottage gardens, farewell wreaths, and quiet offerings left where words would be too large.
flavorText: “Bellflowers sound only in memory.”

name: Lilac
shortDescription: Lilac carries a soft perfume that can make an alley, courtyard, or old letter feel suddenly full of spring. Its blossoms sell quickly to lovers, mourners, perfumers, and anyone trying to make a room forgive them.
flavorText: “Lilac is the scent of remembering kindly.”

name: Amaryllis
shortDescription: Amaryllis rises bold and elegant, with blooms that look too proud for ordinary dirt. It is a gift flower for announcements, apologies, and households that prefer their beauty with a spine of drama.
flavorText: “Amaryllis does not bloom; it makes an entrance.”

name: scarce red bolly balls
shortDescription: Scarce red bolly balls grow in rounded clusters of bright color, more playful than their rarity suggests. Children want them for garlands, herbalists want them for tinctures, and merchants want them before the color fades.
flavorText: “Red bolly balls make a basket look like it is laughing.”

name: Red Marigold
shortDescription: Red Marigold blooms with a warm, ceremonial richness, suited to festival strings, grave offerings, and kitchen gardens that enjoy color with their usefulness. Its petals dry well, which keeps it profitable after the first beauty passes.
flavorText: “Marigolds know how to look festive and practical at once.”

name: Red Tulips
shortDescription: Red Tulips stand smooth, bright, and just dramatic enough to make a simple bouquet feel like a declaration. Their value rises whenever courtship, rivalry, or spring fever needs a cleaner language than speech.
flavorText: “Red tulips say quickly what shy people arrange slowly.”

name: notable wolly
shortDescription: Notable wolly is a fuzzy, curious bloom with soft texture and a name that sounds less formal than its market value. Dyers, charmers, and children all reach for it first, though only the dyers pretend their reasons are professional.
flavorText: “Wolly sells because even serious people like soft things.”

name: scarce round tranquil
shortDescription: Scarce round tranquil is a calm little flower with rounded petals and a color gentle enough to lower voices near the stall. It is used in soothing arrangements, sleep sachets, and gifts meant to apologize without reopening the argument.
flavorText: “Round tranquil is peace small enough to tie with string.”

name: notable jubilance
shortDescription: Notable jubilance is a bright celebratory flower, popular wherever markets need cheering and ceremonies need more color than budget. Its petals almost seem to lean toward music, which is exactly how sellers describe it.
flavorText: “Jubilance is what a flower becomes when it refuses subtlety.”

name: notable sunflower
shortDescription: A notable sunflower turns its broad golden face toward light with such confidence that even tired travelers notice. Its seeds are useful, its color generous, and its height gives humble gardens a little ambition.
flavorText: “Sunflowers teach fields how to look up.”

name: fabled thorn hunter
shortDescription: Fabled thorn hunter is a rare, dangerous plant known for hooked growth and the impression that it notices movement. Botanists speak of it carefully; gardeners speak of it from outside the fence.
flavorText: “A thorn hunter does not chase quickly. It waits where reaching hands become foolish.”

name: yellow bulb
shortDescription: A yellow bulb is humble promise wrapped in papery skin, waiting for soil, water, and patience to make its color visible. Traders sell it to gardeners who understand that beauty often begins looking like an onion.
flavorText: “Bulbs are buried optimism.”

name: Yellow Marigold
shortDescription: Yellow Marigold carries sunlit petals, a sturdy smell, and a cheerful toughness that makes it a market favorite. It brightens shrines, kitchens, and festival tables without pretending to be delicate.
flavorText: “Yellow marigolds are little coins paid to the season.”

name: notable bright red mushrooms
shortDescription: Notable bright red mushrooms are vivid enough to look enchanted, poisonous, delicious, or all three depending on who is selling them. Sensible buyers ask questions; reckless ones admire the color first.
flavorText: “Bright red mushrooms are nature’s way of making curiosity fill out paperwork.”
